divide the other by 12. Add the product of the first and the quotient
of the second. Divide the result by 4. The quotient would reveal

[To work out the formulae given in verses 20-22 first of all
we have to find out the number of jovian years that have elapsed

Jupiter's Course VIII 89
from the beginning of Kali yuga till the beginning of the Saliva-
hana era. This is done as follows;—
In a Mahayuga (i.e. collection of the four yugas) the number
of Solar years is 4320000=3750x1152. That of Jupiter for the
same period is 4370688 = 3794x1152. Hence for every 3750 solar
years we have 3794 jovial years. Therefore 1 solar year=3794=
3750
I-stt? years of Jupiter. Now we have to find out the number of
Jupiter's years past in Kali yuga at the beginning of the Saliva-
hana era. For this proceed thus ;
